title: Sales were up at Tesla but so were costs and spending
content_html:
Tesla posted its financial statement for the second quarter of the year this afternoon. Earlier in July, we learned that the American automaker had had a good quarter in terms of sales, growing 25 percent year over year. Fans hoping that sales increase would result in a plenty profitable Tesla may be disappointed, though. Revenues are up but so are expenses, and the company’s once-enviable double-digit profit margin has fallen to just 1.4 percent.

Tesla brought in $20.5 billion from its electric vehicle business, a 23 percent increase year over year, and just $146 million came from automotive regulatory credits. Credits have been a key to Tesla’s profitability in previous challenging quarters, but they were abolished in the United States with Musk’s blessing in 2025.

There was growth from its energy and storage business, which grew 13 percent year over year to revenues of $3.1 billion, but the most growth was in Tesla’s services, which doubled, bringing in $4.6 billion. Tesla’s shift from a one-time purchase to a monthly subscription for its much-criticized FSD partially automated driver assist—something tied to CEO Elon Musk’s gargantuan remuneration package—was a big help here.

Overall, total revenues were up 26 percent, to $28.2 billion.

But the cost of doing business went up more. Tesla’s operating expenses went up 47 percent to $4.4 billion, and income from those operations fell by 57 percent year over year to $398 million. The company is still profitable—it generated $1.1 billion for the quarter, but that’s 5 percent less than the same three months last year.
content_text: Tesla posted its financial statement for the second quarter of the year this afternoon. Earlier in July, we learned that the American automaker had had a good quarter in terms of sales, growing 25 percent year over year. Fans hoping that sales increase would result in a plenty profitable Tesla may be disappointed, though. Revenues are up but so are expenses, and the company’s once-enviable double-digit profit margin has fallen to just 1.4 percent. Tesla brought in $20.5 billion from its electric vehicle business, a 23 percent increase year over year, and just $146 million came from automotive regulatory credits. Credits have been a key to Tesla’s profitability in previous challenging quarters, but they were abolished in the United States with Musk’s blessing in 2025. There was growth from its energy and storage business, which grew 13 percent year over year to revenues of $3.1 billion, but the most growth was in Tesla’s services, which doubled, bringing in $4.6 billion. Tesla’s shift from a one-time purchase to a monthly subscription for its much-criticized FSD partially automated driver assist—something tied to CEO Elon Musk’s gargantuan remuneration package—was a big help here. Overall, total revenues were up 26 percent, to $28.2 billion. But the cost of doing business went up more. Tesla’s operating expenses went up 47 percent to $4.4 billion, and income from those operations fell by 57 percent year over year to $398 million. The company is still profitable—it generated $1.1 billion for the quarter, but that’s 5 percent less than the same three months last year.

Earlier in July, we learned that the American automaker had had a good quarter in terms of sales, growing 25 percent year over year. Fans hoping that sales increase would result in a plenty profitable Tesla may be disappointed, though. Revenues are up but so are expenses, and the company’s once-enviable double-digit profit margin has fallen to just 1.4 percent.

sentences.en: Tesla posted its financial statement for the second quarter of the year this afternoon.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉今天下午发布了今年第二季度的财务报表。

sentences.en: Earlier in July, we learned that the American automaker had had a good quarter in terms of sales, growing 25 percent year over year.
sentences.zh: 7 月初，我们获悉这家美国汽车制造商的季度销量表现不错，同比增长 25%。

sentences.en: Fans hoping that sales increase would result in a plenty profitable Tesla may be disappointed, though.
sentences.zh: 不过，那些希望特斯拉销量增长能够带来丰厚利润的粉丝可能会失望。

sentences.en: Revenues are up but so are expenses, and the company’s once-enviable double-digit profit margin has fallen to just 1.4 percent.
sentences.zh: 收入增加了，但支出也增加了，该公司曾经令人羡慕的两位数利润率已降至仅 1.4%。

blocks.html_en: Tesla brought in $20.5 billion from its electric vehicle business, a 23 percent increase year over year, and just $146 million came from automotive regulatory credits. Credits have been a key to Tesla’s profitability in previous challenging quarters, but they were abolished in the United States with Musk’s blessing in 2025.

sentences.en: Tesla brought in $20.5 billion from its electric vehicle business, a 23 percent increase year over year, and just $146 million came from automotive regulatory credits.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉从电动汽车业务中获得了 205 亿美元的收入，同比增长 23%，而来自汽车监管信贷的收入仅为 1.46 亿美元。

sentences.en: Credits have been a key to Tesla’s profitability in previous challenging quarters, but they were abolished in the United States with Musk’s blessing in 2025.
sentences.zh: 在之前充满挑战的季度中，积分一直是特斯拉盈利的关键，但在马斯克的支持下，积分在 2025 年在美国被取消。

blocks.html_en: There was growth from its energy and storage business, which grew 13 percent year over year to revenues of $3.1 billion, but the most growth was in Tesla’s services, which doubled, bringing in $4.6 billion. Tesla’s shift from a one-time purchase to a monthly subscription for its much-criticized FSD partially automated driver assist—something tied to CEO Elon Musk’s gargantuan remuneration package—was a big help here.

sentences.en: There was growth from its energy and storage business, which grew 13 percent year over year to revenues of $3.1 billion, but the most growth was in Tesla’s services, which doubled, bringing in $4.6 billion.
sentences.zh: 其能源和存储业务实现了增长，同比增长 13%，营收达到 31 亿美元，但增长最快的是特斯拉的服务，该业务翻了一番，营收达到 46 亿美元。

sentences.en: Tesla’s shift from a one-time purchase to a monthly subscription for its much-criticized FSD partially automated driver assist—something tied to CEO Elon Musk’s gargantuan remuneration package—was a big help here.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉对其备受批评的 FSD 半自动驾驶辅助系统从一次性购买转向按月订阅的转变（这与首席执行官埃隆·马斯克的巨额薪酬方案相关）在这里起到了很大的帮助。

blocks.html_en: Overall, total revenues were up 26 percent, to $28.2 billion.

sentences.en: Overall, total revenues were up 26 percent, to $28.2 billion.
sentences.zh: 总体而言，总收入增长了 26%，达到 282 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: But the cost of doing business went up more. Tesla’s operating expenses went up 47 percent to $4.4 billion, and income from those operations fell by 57 percent year over year to $398 million. The company is still profitable—it generated $1.1 billion for the quarter, but that’s 5 percent less than the same three months last year.

sentences.en: But the cost of doing business went up more.
sentences.zh: 但做生意的成本却上涨了更多。

sentences.en: Tesla’s operating expenses went up 47 percent to $4.4 billion, and income from those operations fell by 57 percent year over year to $398 million.
sentences.zh: 特斯拉的运营费用增长了 47%，达到 44 亿美元，而这些运营收入同比下降 57%，达到 3.98 亿美元。

sentences.en: The company is still profitable—it generated $1.1 billion for the quarter, but that’s 5 percent less than the same three months last year.
sentences.zh: 该公司仍然盈利——该季度创造了 11 亿美元的收入，但这比去年同期的三个月减少了 5%。

content_html_zh:
特斯拉今天下午发布了今年第二季度的财务报表。 7 月初，我们获悉这家美国汽车制造商的季度销量表现不错，同比增长 25%。 不过，那些希望特斯拉销量增长能够带来丰厚利润的粉丝可能会失望。 收入增加了，但支出也增加了，该公司曾经令人羡慕的两位数利润率已降至仅 1.4%。

特斯拉从电动汽车业务中获得了 205 亿美元的收入，同比增长 23%，而来自汽车监管信贷的收入仅为 1.46 亿美元。 在之前充满挑战的季度中，积分一直是特斯拉盈利的关键，但在马斯克的支持下，积分在 2025 年在美国被取消。

其能源和存储业务实现了增长，同比增长 13%，营收达到 31 亿美元，但增长最快的是特斯拉的服务，该业务翻了一番，营收达到 46 亿美元。 特斯拉对其备受批评的 FSD 半自动驾驶辅助系统从一次性购买转向按月订阅的转变（这与首席执行官埃隆·马斯克的巨额薪酬方案相关）在这里起到了很大的帮助。

总体而言，总收入增长了 26%，达到 282 亿美元。

但做生意的成本却上涨了更多。 特斯拉的运营费用增长了 47%，达到 44 亿美元，而这些运营收入同比下降 57%，达到 3.98 亿美元。 该公司仍然盈利——该季度创造了 11 亿美元的收入，但这比去年同期的三个月减少了 5%。
